insight: Master Bot does not understand the value of absorbing damage, suggesting it fails to optimally assign incoming damage to blockers/defenders to preserve key units.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus made an offhand comment that someone should tell Master Bot that absorbing is good, implying the bot undervalues defensive absorption play.

insight: Shalev's Domination Rule (SDR) in Prismata states that for every unit X, it should always be better to have X than to have nothing — units must not be designed so that owning them can be actively harmful. Invulnerability is a known exception because having only invulnerable units lets the opponent choose which to attack, making them sometimes detrimental.
author: mbingo, mrguy888
context: Discussion of Prismata's unit design principles, referencing the official Prismata blog post on Shalev's Rule and how invulnerability breaks it.

insight: In a maximum single-turn damage calculation across all sets, units like Cluster Bolt (50), Venge Cannon (45), Arms Race (44), Grenade Mech (42–46), Antima Comet (22), and base set (40) contribute to a total max damage figure around 463–470, with exact values depending on combo interactions and which units replace others (e.g., Venge Cannon outperforms Savior in damage output at 45 vs 40).
author: mrguy888, zera777, apooche, swapgo
context: Community discussion calculating the theoretical maximum single-turn attack damage achievable across all available Prismata sets, with multiple contributors refining the numbers.

insight: When evaluating units for a maximum damage turn, any unit contributing less than 20 total damage should likely be cut in favor of higher-damage alternatives; Cauterizers are worth 24 damage and Sentinels 30 damage if Antima Comet is already in the set.
author: amalloy
context: amalloy was advising on which units to include or cut when constructing a maximum damage combo, referencing Oxides (12 damage) as too weak and comparing Cauterizers and Sentinels.
